commentaries.author: Ambrose of Milan
commentaries.content: Let our course take us to regions above, because it is better to ascend. Finally, as was read today, “Woe to them that go down to Egypt.” Surely it is not wrong to go to Egypt, but to change to the ways of the Egyptians, to change to the violence of their treachery and to the ugliness of their wantonness—this is wrong. He that changes in this way descends, and one who descends falls. Let us keep away, then, from the Egyptian who is a man, but [let us] not [keep away] from God. Even the king of Egypt himself fell under the dominion of his own vices and in comparison with him Moses was accounted a god, ruling over kingdoms and subjecting powers to himself. So we read that it was said to Moses: “I shall make you a god to Pharaoh.”

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Chariots. He continues to inveigh against this practice, chap. xxx. 16. (Calmet)
Salmanasar will ruin the ten tribes. (Menochius)
